name: Pecan-Stuffed Flank Steak
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup bread cubes (torn from day-old bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the flank steak for stuffing by scoring it in a diamond pattern on both sides with a sharp knife, cutting no deeper than 1/2 inch.
2. Pound the steak on both sides with a meat mallet until it is thin enough to roll.
3. In a skillet, cook the onion and mushrooms in hot butter for 2 minutes. Turn off the heat and add pecans, parsley, bread cubes, salt, pepper, ground cardamom, ginger, cloves, and egg. Mix well and cool.
4. Season the flank steak with salt and pepper. Spread the cooled stuffing over the pounded meat, leaving a 1/2-inch border on all sides.
5. Starting on one long side of the steak, roll it closed and tie at 2-inch intervals with kitchen string. Tie lengthwise if needed to hold the filling in place.
6. Brown the rolled steak on all sides in a teaspoon of margarine in an oven-proof casserole. Add orange juice and beef bouillon (canned or made with a bouillon cube). Cover the pan and roast at 350°F for 1½-2 hours, turning once halfway through. If the pan dries out, add liquid.
7. When the roll is tender (poke it with a skewer), move it to a serving platter, remove the strings, and slice. Strain the pan juices if desired, and pour over the meat. Garnish the platter with parsley.
8. This dish is also good served cold. Allow it to cool completely before slicing. Omit the pan juice when serving cold.
